<!-- Generated by deep-research workflow (6 agents) on 2026-06-20. Source: ARC personal report audit + 5 expert perspectives. -->

# ARC Personal Report: Talent Acquisition vs Talent Development - differentiation + enhancement plan

Prepared for the VIFM product owner by the lead consultant. Scope: the ARC Personal AI Readiness report (four factors - AI Sense-Check, AI Working Practice, AI Collaboration, AI Adaptive Mindset) as rendered on-screen and in the EN/AR PDFs, and how it should diverge by `talent_lens`.

---

## 1. Executive summary

The ARC Personal report runs off a single four-factor instrument but is asked to serve two opposite decisions, and today it does not really make the split. The `acquisition` lens is a genuinely distinct hiring report - it swaps in present-tense factor reads, adds a verdict / calibration / strengths / interview-probe analysis block, and strips out coaching and course recommendations. But the `development` lens is, in effect, the legacy generic snapshot with two relabelled header strings ("Talent Development" instead of "General") and a `?lens=development` query param; its factor descriptions, coaching, courses and next-steps are byte-for-byte identical to the no-lens path. So we currently ship one specialised report and one report that only pretends to be specialised.

"Identical" is a real problem, not a cosmetic one. A hiring report and a development report are built for different readers making different decisions under different legal exposure: the hiring report is a gate scrutinised by a candidate with every incentive to challenge an adverse outcome under UAE/Saudi PDPL; the development report is a mirror handed to someone already inside the tent. The feedback literature (Kluger and DeNisi's Feedback Intervention Theory) shows that selection-style language - cut-scores, "below standard", percentile ranks - in a development context shifts attention to the self and can actively depress performance, the exact failure mode a development report is engineered to avoid. Conversely, development-style content on a hiring report (course plans, coaching narratives, succession framing) muddies the at-will line, invites the reader to make a training decision instead of a hiring one, and buries the one thing a hiring manager needs: a defensible, human-gated recommendation. Because both reports are generated from the same data, the cheapest high-value wins are presentation and framing changes we can ship without collecting anything new; the bigger builds (role benchmarks, cut-scores, defensible norms, prior-run deltas) are where the genuine differentiation and defensibility live. This report specifies both, with a prioritised, file-level backlog.

---

## 2. The core difference in one table

| Dimension | Talent Acquisition (hiring) | Talent Development (growth) |
|---|---|---|
| **Purpose** | Gate: is this person safe and sufficient for *this* role, now? | Mirror: where am I now, where next, what is the first step? |
| **Audience** | Hiring manager / recruiter / client (a third party deciding about a stranger) | The individual + their manager / development partner (the beneficiary) |
| **Reader's decision** | Advance / Review / Hold on a candidacy | Which 2-3 priorities to invest in, and how |
| **Framing (normative vs ipsative)** | Normative + role-referenced (vs a bar, vs a pool) | Ipsative (vs own baseline and own target); norms demoted to faint context |
| **Comparison basis** | Role threshold (primary), defensible norm percentile (when n supports), shortlist rank (convenience) | Baseline → current → target trajectory; gap-to-target, not percentile |
| **Predictive vs formative** | Predictive: states what scores forecast + confidence band + method-validity note | Formative: states the next rung and the next rep, not a forecast |
| **Distortion handling** | Surfaced on the face: self-vs-objective gap read adversarially as an over-claim / reliability flag; verify via probes | Surfaced gently as a coaching target / confidence gap; awareness is step one |
| **Course recs** | Omit (you do not train a non-employee; implies a hiring commitment) | Central: gap-anchored, curated short-list per priority, bound to enrolment |
| **Development plan** | Replace with "verify before deciding" structured interview probes | 30/60/90 plan, 70-20-10 tagged, deliberate-practice reps, SMART commitments |
| **Re-measure cadence** | Freshness stamp + short shelf-life ("re-screen if requisition > 3-6 months") | Recurring re-measure (quarterly micro-loop / 6-12 month full); show trajectory |
| **Language / tone** | Decision-first, evidence-bounded, probabilistic, job-related; no clinical or absolute language | Strengths-first, growth-mindset, "not yet" / "area to grow"; supportive, narrative |
| **Legal exposure** | High: job-relatedness, 4/5ths adverse impact, proxy discrimination, PDPL automated-decision rights, audit trail | Low: beneficiary processing, no selection event, no adverse decision to defend |
| **What to omit** | Coaching, courses, succession/career-pathing, raw personality-as-truth, aspirational tone | Global verdict, cut-scores / pass-fail, peer-rank headline, risk/"concern" framing, decision-oriented audit apparatus |

---

## 3. Talent ACQUISITION report - section by section

The acquisition lens is already the stronger of the two. The work here is to make it *defensible and role-referenced*, not just "different from development". Today it has a deterministic verdict, a calibration sentence, strengths, dev-areas with interview probes, profile shape and a basis caveat - good bones. Gaps: it has no role bar, no confidence framing, a single thin over-claim signal, and percentiles that exist on screen but never reach the PDF.

**3.0 Lead with the recommendation + guardrail (NEW, top of report).** Today the report leads with the overall score card. Re-order so the first thing the reader sees is a 3-band advisory - **Advance / Review / Hold** - with a one-line rationale and the decision basis (which factors drove it). Crucially, print the guardrail on the report face: "This is a screening signal. A qualified human makes the hiring decision; this score does not auto-disqualify." This satisfies the automated-decision provisions of UAE Decree-Law 45/2021 and the Saudi PDPL, and it mirrors the no-reject stance already in `prehire/scoring.ts` (`advance/review/hold/incomplete`, never reject). The band derives from the existing `verdict` plus per-factor bar checks (3.2) - no new data needed to ship a first version off the maturity stage; the role-bar version comes with the bigger build.

**3.1 Overall score - demote it, do not headline a composite.** Per the AI-readiness expert, a single "AI readiness 3.4/5" lets a strong AI Working Practice mask a disqualifying AI Sense-Check gap - the exact failure the report exists to catch. Keep the overall as context, but make the per-factor sufficiency table (3.2) the headline. Also fix the scoring bug noted in the audit: the overall is an unweighted mean of four factor means in `page.tsx` / `route.tsx`, and a factor with zero answered items contributes 0, silently dragging overall and stage down. For a hiring verdict this is a defensibility liability (you cannot explain a score the candidate did not earn). Exclude unanswered factors from the mean and surface "not enough evidence on factor X" explicitly.

**3.2 Per-factor sufficiency vs role bar (NEW - the core change).** Replace the four descriptive factor cards with a four-row table: **factor | role bar | candidate score | Below bar / At bar / Above bar | one-line "so what"**. Role-weight the factors per the role profile, because the four do not gate equally:
- **AI Sense-Check** gates any role where AI output feeds a decision, document or client deliverable (analysts, advisors, regulated finance). A candidate strong on AI Working Practice but weak on AI Sense-Check is *more* dangerous here, not less.
- **AI Working Practice** gates high-throughput individual-contributor roles where the productivity case is the job; contextual for senior roles that direct others' use.
- **AI Collaboration** inverts with seniority: gating for team leads and "AI champion" roles, contextual for a heads-down specialist.
- **AI Adaptive Mindset** is a near-universal soft floor (it predicts whether a below-bar candidate closes the gap fast) and is the most durable factor over time.
Until role benchmarks exist (bigger build), ship an interim version using the existing maturity-stage thresholds as a generic bar and label it "indicative bar, not role-calibrated".

**3.3 Predictive note + confidence band (NEW).** Add a short, plain-language "what this predicts" line with a confidence state (high / moderate / near-threshold) and a one-line method-validity note ("structured assessment of this kind predicts later performance moderately well; it reduces, not eliminates, hiring risk"). A factor score within a small margin of its bar should read as *near-threshold / indeterminate*, which triggers the probes in 3.5 rather than a clean pass. We have the scaffolding in `src/lib/scoring/reliability.ts` to express bands; this is framing plus a threshold check, not new measurement.

**3.4 Response validity / distortion - widen and keep it adversarial.** Today's only distortion detector is one aggregate self-vs-objective delta (the `calibration` block in `personal-analysis.ts`, ±0.75 threshold, gated at objectiveCount >= 4), and it is read constructively. For hiring, read it adversarially and give it its own "Response validity" panel with a traffic-light state: *Consistent / within range*, *Elevated impression management - interpret elevated scores with caution*, *Inconsistent responding - verify or re-administer*. Anchor the hiring verdict on the **objective** score, not the self-rating; use the self-rating only to compute the over-claim gap. The largest positive self-minus-objective gaps on AI Working Practice and AI Sense-Check are the strongest over-reliance predictors and should name *which* scores to discount. (A true per-item lie-scale / inconsistency index is a bigger build; the aggregate gap is shippable now and already computed.)

**3.5 AI-specific risk flags (NEW - hiring only).** Add a dedicated "Risk flags for hiring consideration" box, each phrased as a *signal to probe*, never an auto-reject:
- **Over-reliance / automation complacency**: high AI Working Practice paired with low AI Sense-Check. Each factor alone looks fine; the risk is in the gap. For finance/regulated GCC roles this is the single most important flag.
- **Unsafe / shadow-AI use**: low responsible-posture signal within AI Adaptive Mindset, often with high AI Working Practice - propensity to paste confidential/PII data into public tools. A UAE/Saudi PDPL compliance liability, not just a skill gap; flag the propensity and tell the manager to probe it.
- **Over-claiming**: the calibration gap from 3.4 reported as its own signal.
These are computable today from the four factor scores plus the self/objective split.

**3.6 Verify before deciding - keep and strengthen the probes.** The existing `FACTOR_PROBE` interview probes are the report's best feature. Strengthen by adding **rating anchors** (what a strong vs weak answer looks like) per probe - structured-interview validity comes from standardized *evaluation*, not just standardized questions. Trigger a probe set for any factor that is below-bar, near-threshold, or faking-suspect, and tie each probe to the evidence gap, not the person.

**3.7 Fairness, limitations and audit reference (NEW footer).** A short "job-relatedness note" (why these factors matter for this role), a "limitations of this assessment" honesty statement, and a reference that an immutable audit record exists (`prehire_audit_log` shape). Individual demographics never appear here; 4/5ths adverse-impact lives in the aggregate fairness hub, not on the individual report.

**3.8 Freshness stamp (NEW).** AI readiness ages in months, not years (tools and norms move; AI Working Practice decays fastest, AI Adaptive Mindset slowest). Stamp the report "as measured on [date]; re-screen if the requisition is older than ~3-6 months."

**OMIT under acquisition (already largely correct):** course recommendations, "Where to focus next" coaching, the "Maps to VIFM AC" developmental line, 30/60/90 plans, succession/career framing. Keep them gated off as today.

---

## 4. Talent DEVELOPMENT report - section by section

This is where the biggest gap is: the development lens has *no* development-specific analytical content today. Everything below is net-new framing on data we already compute. The governing principle (Feedback Intervention Theory): keep attention on the task and on learning, off the self.

**4.0 Framing / psychological contract (NEW, top).** Add a purpose box: "This is a development report. It is confidential to you and your development partner. It is not a scorecard, not a ranking against colleagues, and not an input to any pay, promotion, or hiring decision." Stating the non-uses removes the evaluative threat that drives FIT's negative effects. Add a one-line growth-mindset preamble and the scale legend. This is a content/template change in `personal-snapshot.tsx`, the AR HTML, and `page.tsx`.

**4.1 Strengths first (CHANGE ordering).** Open with 2-3 signature strengths with concrete behavioural evidence before any gap. This is a psychological-safety primer and a strengths-as-lever move. We already compute strengths in `personal-analysis.ts` (the `strengths` list with `FACTOR_DESCRIPTIVE` reads) but only build that block under acquisition - reuse it for development with a development-framed read, and put it first.

**4.2 Ipsative core: you vs your own baseline and target (NEW - the defining section).** Replace the absolute factor scores with a three-marker track per factor: **Baseline (prior run, or this run as Time 1) - Current - Target**. Headline the gap-to-target and the delta-from-baseline, not a percentile. Demote norms to faint "typical range for this role family" context explicitly labelled "context, not verdict". The target defaults to 4 (already the recommender target). Baseline/delta needs the prior-run plumbing (bigger build); ship the "current vs target" version now and light up the delta when reassessment data exists.

**4.3 Prioritised development priorities (NEW - replaces flat factor list).** Pick **2-3 priorities** on impact x leverage (gap-to-target weighted by centrality), sequenced not flat. For each: a one-line "why this one now". For AI readiness specifically, sequence AI Adaptive Mindset and AI Sense-Check first (you cannot safely scale use you cannot evaluate, and you will not relearn without openness), then AI Working Practice throughput, then AI Collaboration reach. Distinguish skill gaps (need practice) from exposure gaps (need a different assignment).

**4.4 Personalised plan: 30/60/90 + 70-20-10 + deliberate practice (NEW).** Per priority, tag every action 70 (experiential stretch assignment) / 20 (social - named mentor, peer, manager cadence) / 10 (formal course). Name one repeatable weekly rep per priority (deliberate practice: specific sub-skill, edge of ability, immediate feedback, repetition). Stage across 30/60/90 windows with 2-3 actions max and a checkpoint each. Reuse the Learning Plan PDF's existing 30/60/90 roadmap pattern.

**4.5 Learning pathways tied to each gap (KEEP, sharpen).** The course recommender (`recommendCoursesForIndividualSnapshot`) already runs for development/null. Sharpen from a list into per-priority curation: one best course + one resource + one on-the-job mechanism, each with a one-clause "why this for this gap". Sequence the 10% formal input *before* the 70% application window.

**4.6 Manager coaching guide (NEW, manager-facing page).** Arm the manager with a GROW agenda (Goal / Reality / Options / Will) and 3-4 ready question stems per priority, plus FIT-grounded do's and don'ts (task-focused not person-focused; never a gap without a next step; one or two priorities per conversation) and psychological-safety opener prompts. Candid coaching notes live on this manager-only page; the individual-facing pages stay strengths-forward.

**4.7 Self-reflection + goal-setting + accountability (NEW).** Reflection prompts ("which of these resonates / surprises you? what is one thing you will start this week?"), a self-authored SMART commitment with an implementation intention ("when X arises, I will do Y"), a named development partner, a check-in cadence, and a visible next-review date with a signature line - converting the document into a contract.

**4.8 Longitudinal re-measurement (NEW).** State the cadence in the report (quarterly micro-loop for behaviours in active practice; 6-12 month full re-assessment). On re-run, headline movement (baseline → prior → current arrows per factor) and celebrate it explicitly ("+1 on AI Collaboration since last cycle"). Honour the ipsative promise even on a dip - frame a plateau as data for re-planning, not failure. AI readiness justifies a *shorter* cadence than stable competencies because both the person and the bar move; a flat absolute score can represent real growth against a rising field.

**Self-vs-objective for development:** surface the same calibration the acquisition lens uses, but read it constructively. Self-high/objective-low is a starting conversation (common on AI Working Practice - people conflate "I use ChatGPT" with skilled use), not a red flag; the move is to make the gap visible gently and target the verification habit. Self-low/objective-high is a confidence gap worth encouraging (especially on AI Collaboration). Today development/null readers see no calibration at all - this is a pure framing add on data already computed.

**OMIT under development:** the global verdict, cut-scores / pass-fail / "below bar", peer-rank headline, risk/"concern" framing, and the decision-oriented audit/freshness apparatus. Wall the development report off from any selection/Pre-Hire use (a policy/RLS decision) so candour is never punished by selection consequences.

---

## 5. Shared enhancements that improve both

1. **Push percentiles and Mode-C cohort deltas into the PDF.** Today `computePersonalNorms()` (overall + per-factor percentiles) and `computeWorkforceReadiness()` cohort means are computed only in `page.tsx` and never reach either PDF renderer - the downloaded PDF has no percentile or cohort delta. Pass them into `personal-snapshot.tsx` and `personal-snapshot-ar-html.ts`. (Acquisition uses percentile as a role-pool anchor when n supports; development demotes it to faint context.) Keep the existing honesty gate (`MIN_NORM_SAMPLE`) and the "accruing" note.
2. **Fix the unanswered-factor scoring bug** in both `page.tsx` and `route.tsx`: an empty factor scores 0 and drags the unweighted overall down. Exclude unanswered factors and surface "insufficient evidence" instead. Matters for both lenses but is a defensibility must for acquisition.
3. **Make the lens a real first-class branch.** Today only `isAcquisition` exists; `development` and `null` are indistinguishable. Introduce explicit `isDevelopment` handling so development gets its own builder (the §4 content), and decide what `null`/"General" should default to (recommend: default to the development experience, since the generic snapshot is developmental in spirit).
4. **EN/AR parity for all new content.** Every new string lands in both `personal-snapshot.tsx` (EN/React-PDF) and `personal-snapshot-ar-html.ts` (AR/Puppeteer), plus the on-screen `page.tsx`. Arabic must be human-reviewed, not placeholder MT - in the GCC, a poorly translated item is a fairness grievance (English-heavy scoring partly measures English proficiency, which tracks nationality).
5. **Methodology page per lens.** The acquisition methodology cites selection validity + 4/5ths + cut-score rationale; the development methodology cites FIT, deliberate practice, 70-20-10, growth mindset. Branch the existing methodology link by lens.
6. **Tool-agnostic, scenario-based items audit (construct cleanup).** Items that assume a specific consumer AI product measure exposure (a proxy for age/nationality), not readiness. This improves both reports and is a fairness prerequisite before any acquisition gating.

---

| # | Item | Lens | Effort | Impact | Files / data + new data needed |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| B1 | Role benchmarks / per-factor role bars + gating-vs-contextual weighting | TA | L | High | Role-profile data bound to the snapshot (`role_profiles` / `role_profile_competencies` pattern, `competency-gap.ts`); new: role-calibrated minimums per factor per role family, human-reviewed |
| B2 | Documented cut-scores with rationale (modified-Angoff / criterion data) | TA | L | High | New cut-score store + rationale; surfaces in §3.2/§3.7; needed before any real gating |
| B3 | Defensible norm group at adequate n; role-pool percentiles in PDF | both | L | High | `computePersonalNorms()` + `MIN_NORM_SAMPLE`; new: accumulate ~200-500 completed snapshots, named norm groups |
| B4 | Prior-run / reassessment deltas (baseline → prior → current) | TD | L | High | New: personal `prior_assessment_id` link + year-on-year read (today there is none on the personal side); reuse AC `prior_candidate_id` / "↑+1 vs prior" pattern |
| B5 | Per-item validity scales (social-desirability, inconsistency, over-claim) | TA | L | Med | New item-level keying beyond the single aggregate self-vs-objective delta; psychometrics validity-flag pattern |
| B6 | 30/60/90 + 70-20-10 deliberate-practice plan generator, enrolment-bound | TD | M | High | `recommendCoursesForIndividualSnapshot`, Learning Plan PDF roadmap pattern, Academy enrolment; new: 70-20-10 action templates per factor |
| B7 | Manager coaching guide page (GROW stems + FIT do/don'ts) | TD | M | Med | New manager-only render section; access-walled from selection use (RLS/policy) |
| B8 | Immutable audit reference + freshness/version-pin on acquisition report | TA | M | Med | `prehire_audit_log` pattern; version-pin question bank + cut-scores + role mapping per run |
| B9 | Tool-agnostic item rewrite (remove construct-irrelevant exposure proxies) | both | M | Med | ARA question bank items; new SME/human review pass, EN/AR parity |

---

## 7. Data we already have vs data we'd need

**Already computed and available (these power every quick win):**
- Per-factor scores for all four factors (AI Sense-Check, AI Working Practice, AI Collaboration, AI Adaptive Mindset) and the overall mean - `page.tsx` / `route.tsx`.
- The self-rating vs objective split (`selfAvg`, `objectiveAvg`, `objCount`) - computed every run, today surfaced only in the acquisition calibration sentence. This is enough for: the response-validity panel, the over-claim flag, and the constructive development calibration.
- Maturity stage (emerging / practising / embedded), overall and per-factor.
- The deterministic acquisition analysis (`buildPersonalAnalysis`): verdict, profile shape, calibration, strengths, development areas, `FACTOR_PROBE` interview probes, basis caveat - reusable for the development builder and the new acquisition band.
- Norm percentiles (`computePersonalNorms` → overall + per-factor, gated at `MIN_NORM_SAMPLE`) and Mode-C cohort means (`computeWorkforceReadiness`) - both on screen, both currently absent from the PDFs.
- Course recommendations (`recommendCoursesForIndividualSnapshot`, target 4, limit 5) for development/null.
- Respondent name/email, language preference, `talent_lens`, `engagement_stage`, `include_individual_layer`, generated date; static fact-sheet rows; methodology PDF link.

**Would need to collect or model (these gate the bigger builds):**
- **Role benchmarks / per-factor cut-scores** - there is no role bar on the personal report today; B1/B2 need role-calibrated minimums per factor per role family, human-reviewed and documented, before any defensible acquisition gating.
- **A defensible norm group at adequate n** - percentiles exist but the honesty gate means they only become claimable at ~200-500 completed snapshots with named, role-appropriate reference samples (B3). Until then: raw band + "indicative, no local norm".
- **Prior-run / reassessment data** - there is none on the personal side today (unlike the org ARA report). B4 needs a personal prior-link and a year-on-year read before the development trajectory section can show real deltas; ship "current vs target" first and light up deltas later.
- **Item-level validity scales** - today the only distortion detector is one aggregate self-vs-objective delta (gated at objectiveCount >= 4). A real social-desirability / inconsistency / per-item over-claim scale (B5) needs new keyed items.
- **70-20-10 action templates and enrolment binding** - per-factor experiential/social/formal action libraries (B6) and the manager GROW content (B7) are new content, not new measurement.
- **Audit / version-pin substrate for acquisition** - the `prehire_audit_log` shape exists in the platform; wiring the personal acquisition report to a version-pinned, audited run (B8) is integration work plus a question-bank version stamp.

Net: roughly two-thirds of the differentiation can ship on data we already compute (Section 6 quick wins), and the remaining defensibility-critical third (role bars, cut-scores, defensible norms, prior-run deltas) is a clearly bounded data-collection and modelling programme.
